what_it_does: This JSON file is the voice-trial manifest: it records the concrete persona-to-engine-to-port wiring, the voice-argument semantics each engine expects, and whether a shared reference clip is required, so a launcher/verifier can iterate the five trial voices.
observations: Defines six engine ports: kokoro 4123, chatterbox 4124, orpheus 4125, cosyvoice 4126, xtts 4127, qwen3tts 4128.
Maps five personas to engines: Viv/chatterbox, Tess/cosyvoice, Sable/qwen3tts, Pip/orpheus, Wren/xtts.
Sable is marked as the TRIAL STARTER because qwen3tts needs no reference clip.
Three personas (Viv, Tess, Wren) depend on COMPANY_VOICE_REF, which the file says does not yet exist.
The reference clip is planned to be generated post-restart by qwen3tts from the base voice description via voice/ops/make_reference_clip.sh.
voice_arg semantics vary by engine: text description, named voice bank entry, or reference-clip path.
XTTS is explicitly flagged as non-commercial weights and trial-only.
Orpheus carries a VRAM/co-residency warning with the chat brain on a 16GB card.
The shared base voice is described as a refined, educated Australian woman in her early forties.
standouts: The trial cannot fully start until a missing reference clip is generated, blocking three of five personas.
Sable/qwen3tts is the only persona that can be brought up immediately because it synthesizes the voice from text alone.
XTTS is singled out with a non-commercial license restriction, limiting it to the by-ear trial.
Orpheus is flagged as the heaviest engine and may conflict with the chat brain for GPU memory.
conventions: Uses _doc and _generated metadata fields to explain provenance and scope.
Keeps persona brain/voice description out of the manifest and only records runtime wiring.
Assigns contiguous ports per engine for easy iteration.
Uses null voice_arg to mean 'fall back to COMPANY_VOICE_REF' for ref-clip engines.
Mixes structured config with free-text operational notes and warnings.
concerns: A majority of the trial depends on a reference clip that is documented as not yet existing.
voice_arg values mix literal strings, null, and runtime placeholders like 'persona.voice_description'.
XTTS is wired into the trial despite being explicitly non-production.
Orpheus hardware warning suggests a deployment constraint that is not encoded in the config itself.
There is no schema or version field beyond the human-readable _generated string.
notes: This is the trial wiring sheet: which character speaks through which TTS engine on which port and what each engine needs as a voice argument.
questions: How is the COMPANY_VOICE_REF clip validated for consistency with the base voice description before it is used by three engines?
What fallback or gating exists if qwen3tts reference generation fails or the output is unsuitable?
How does the launcher enforce the non-commercial XTTS restriction in practice?
What happens if the named Orpheus voice 'tara' is unavailable or renamed in the voice bank?
Are engine_ports intended to be environment-specific or hard-coded for this trial?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a trial config/wiring file matches the actual content: persona-to-engine mappings, port assignments, voice-argument semantics, and reference-clip dependency plan.
apparent_themes: Separation of voice identity design from runtime engine wiring.
Use of a single synthetic reference clip as a consistency anchor across multiple clone engines.
Incremental bring-up by starting with the engine that has no external dependency.
Explicit licensing and hardware guardrails embedded in configuration comments.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to coordinate a multi-engine voice comparison trial where one designed identity is reproduced across diverse TTS systems, while making dependencies, licensing limits, and hardware risks explicit.
novelty: It treats a generated reference clip as a shared synthetic identity anchor across three different clone engines, and uses one engine (qwen3tts) to bootstrap that anchor from a text description rather than from a human recording.
contribution: A concise, engine-agnostic trial wiring table that records persona-to-engine mappings, port assignments, voice-argument semantics, and reference-clip dependencies, enabling a launcher to iterate the five trial configurations without hard-coding them elsewhere.
summary_for_embedding: company/voice/trial_manifest.json is a voice-trial configuration manifest that maps five persona characters to TTS engines, ports, voice-argument kinds, and shared reference-clip requirements for a by-ear comparison.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to give the launcher and verification tooling a single, concrete wiring table for bringing up each trial voice independently.
